__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus datasource scraping the redpanda-operator /metrics endpoint.

description: Redpanda operator full-stack observability — multicluster raft, StretchCluster member status, and operator reconcile health. Each panel description ends with a How-to-read hint summarising the healthy state and what spikes / drops mean. See docs/operator-metrics.md for the metric inventory.

panels.title: Current leader
panels.description:
last_over_time(operator_multicluster_raft_state{state="leader"}[1m]) == 1, evaluated as an instant query so the panel shows the leader at "now" rather than every peer that was ever leader during the dashboard time range. The 1-minute window drops stale series from killed peers.

How to read: one peer name = healthy. Empty = no leader right now (cluster is mid-election or wedged). Two or more would mean two peers were leader within the last minute — only realistic during a fresh transition.

panels.title: Raft term
panels.description:
operator_multicluster_raft_term. Monotonic counter that ticks every election (successful or not). After steady state, all peers agree on the same value.

How to read: stable value = healthy. Rapid jumps = leader churn / election storms.

panels.title: Leader changes (last 10m)
panels.description:
increase(operator_multicluster_raft_leader_changes_total[10m]) — total leader transitions observed over a rolling 10-minute window. A single transition (e.g. after killing the leader pod) lands as 1 and decays back to 0 after ~10m.

How to read: 0 = healthy. 1–2 = normal pod restart / rolling upgrade. Sustained ≥ 3 = flapping (network instability or load on the leader).

panels.title: Send latency p99 (per peer, leader's view)
panels.description:
histogram_quantile(0.99, ...) of operator_multicluster_raft_send_duration_seconds, filtered to the current leader (so only one perspective is shown). Cross-region RTT dispersion is the headline cross-cluster perf signal.

How to read: p99 < ~100ms is great for cross-region. < 500ms is acceptable. > 1s sustained means the cross-cluster network is degraded.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, peer) (rate(operator_multicluster_raft_send_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) * on(instance) group_left() (operator_multicluster_raft_state{state="leader"} == 1))

panels.title: Send queue length (per sender → destination)
panels.description:
operator_multicluster_raft_send_queue_length — every peer emits its own view of every destination peer (including itself, queue length 0), so each (sender, destination) pair gets its own series. Legend reads sender → destination.

How to read: 0 is good. Sustained > 0 on a (sender → destination) means that sender's queue to that destination is filling up — the destination peer can't drain at the rate raft is producing, usually because the cross-cluster link is slow or the destination is GC-thrashing.

panels.title: Messages sent / received rate (heartbeats, append)
panels.description:
rate of operator_multicluster_raft_messages_{sent,received}_total restricted to msg_type=heartbeat|append. Steady-state cluster shows a regular cadence (~10 heartbeats/s per peer pair by default).

How to read: regular flat lines for heartbeats = healthy. Append spikes during config writes are normal. Sudden flat-line on heartbeats = peer disconnected.

panels.title: Send errors (last 5m, by class)
panels.description:
increase(operator_multicluster_raft_send_errors_total[5m]) by peer and error class — total errors of each class accumulated in a rolling 5-minute window. Reported as a count rather than ops/s because errors are typically rare events; the rolling count reads more naturally (e.g. "25 errors" rather than "0.083 ops/s"). Classes: timeout (DeadlineExceeded — peer silent / slow), canceled (Canceled — graceful shutdown), unavailable (peer down / dial failure), auth (TLS / RBAC misconfig), marshal (local serialisation bug), other (anything else).

How to read: 0 is healthy. Any sustained non-zero count points at the matching root cause — timeout = network or slow peer, unavailable = peer dead, auth = TLS rotation / RBAC misconfig.

panels.title: Follower match-lag entries (leader's view)
panels.description:
operator_multicluster_raft_follower_match_lag_entries × leader marker, aggregated to one series per peer. Leader's last log index minus each follower's matched index. last_over_time(...[1m]) on the leader filter drops the stale series after a leader pod is killed.

How to read: 0 on every peer = healthy. Climbing on a peer = that follower is falling behind the leader's log. A few entries of lag are fine during a write burst; thousands and growing = problem.

panels.title: Peer reachability + dropped messages
panels.description:
operator_multicluster_raft_peer_reachable (0/1) overlaid with rate(operator_multicluster_raft_messages_dropped_total[5m]). Each peer emits its own view of every other peer, so the legend reads sender → destination.

How to read: reachable=1 always + dropped=0 always = healthy. reachable=0 means that sender's last attempt to that destination failed (transient or sustained). Drops correlate with reachable=0 windows — when a peer is unreachable, EnqueueSend can't fit messages into the per-peer queue and they're dropped (raft retransmits on the next tick, so isolated drops are harmless; sustained drops mean a peer is chronically unreachable).

panels.title: Leadership history
panels.description:
Status timeline of which peer was the raft leader over time. Each row is one vCluster peer; coloured segments mark the windows where that peer reported state=leader=1. Aggregated with max by (vcluster) so pod restarts (which create new instance/pod label sets while reusing the vcluster external label) appear on a single row.

How to read: a single long green bar on one peer = stable leadership. Stripes across multiple peers = leader flapping (correlate with the Send latency / Send errors panels to find the cause).

panels.title: Member reachability (per StretchCluster, per member)
panels.description:
max by (stretchcluster, member) (operator_stretchcluster_member_reachable). Aggregated so the panel shows one line per (stretchcluster, member) — each peer emits its own view of every member, the aggregation dedupes them.

How to read: 1 on every member = healthy. 0 on a member = that peer's apiserver is unreachable from this operator (real outage or transient hiccup). Brief dips are normal; sustained 0 means a peer is genuinely down.

panels.title: Broker shortfall (desired − ready, per member)
panels.description:
max by (stretchcluster, member) (operator_stretchcluster_brokers − operator_stretchcluster_brokers_ready). The gap between desired and ready broker counts per member.

How to read: 0 on every member = healthy. A spike > 0 means brokers on that member are unscheduled, crashlooping, or failing readiness. Expected briefly during scale-up or rolling restarts; sustained > 0 means a pod won't come up.

panels.title: Spec drift (per member)
panels.description:
max by (stretchcluster, member) (operator_stretchcluster_spec_drift). 1 means that member's local StretchCluster.spec differs from the operator's view; 0 means they match.

How to read: 0 on every member = healthy. A 1 on any member means someone applied a stale manifest to that peer and reconciliation is blocked across all peers until the spec is realigned. Re-apply the canonical manifest to clear.

panels.title: Replication health (per StretchCluster)
panels.description:
max by (stretchcluster) (operator_stretchcluster_replication_health). Cluster-wide replication health from the admin API.

How to read: 1 = healthy (all brokers up, no under-replicated partitions). 0 = unhealthy; check broker readiness and the admin API directly (rpk cluster health) for the specific cause.

panels.title: Reconcile rate (per controller)
panels.description:
controller_runtime_reconcile_total / sec, summed by controller.

How to read: low and steady (typically under 1/s per controller once the system has converged) = healthy. Sustained high rate on a stable cluster = controller is spinning — cross-check the Steady-state-vs-total panel to confirm.

panels.title: Reconcile error rate (per controller)
panels.description:
controller_runtime_reconcile_errors_total / sec, summed by controller.

How to read: 0 = healthy. Sustained > 0.1/s on any controller = that controller is failing reconciles consistently; check the operator pod logs and the relevant resource's status conditions.

panels.title: Reconcile duration p99 (per controller)
panels.description:
histogram_quantile(0.99, ...) of controller_runtime_reconcile_time_seconds.

How to read: sub-second is healthy for most controllers. Climbing p99 = controller is slowing down (admin API call, large resource list, slow apiserver, etc.). Sustained > a few seconds = something is wrong.

panels.title: Steady-state vs total reconciles
panels.description:
Two rates per controller: total reconciles vs reconciles that returned (Result{}, nil) — i.e. no work to do, no re-queue requested.

How to read: lines should track each other after the initial convergence. A persistent gap (total > steady) means reconciles are repeatedly returning Result{RequeueAfter: ...}, i.e. work-in-progress. If total stays high while steady stays flat, the controller is spinning without making progress.

panels.title: Workqueue depth (per controller)
panels.description:
workqueue_depth — items waiting to be reconciled per controller.

How to read: oscillates near 0 = healthy. Sustained growth = controller can't keep up; increase MaxConcurrentReconciles or investigate per-reconcile latency.

panels.title: Active workers / max workers
panels.description:
controller_runtime_active_workers ÷ controller_runtime_max_concurrent_reconciles per controller.

How to read: < 1.0 most of the time = pool has headroom. Pegged at 1.0 sustained = saturation; per-reconcile latency is the bottleneck.

panels.title: Workqueue retries rate (per controller)
panels.description:
workqueue_retries_total / sec per controller — controller-runtime re-queues an item every time Reconcile returns an error or Result.Requeue=true.

How to read: 0 or near-0 = healthy. Sustained non-zero = controller can't make progress on something; correlate with the Reconcile error rate panel to confirm.

panels.title: Seconds since last steady-state reconcile (per controller)
panels.description:
time() - operator_controller_reconcile_last_success_timestamp_seconds — how long ago each controller last reached steady state (either a (Result{}, nil) return or a periodic-requeue matching the controller's configured defaultRequeueTimeout). The underlying gauge is set by the observability wrapper inside the steady-state branch; subtracting from time() at query time gives 'seconds since last success' without any goroutine bookkeeping.

How to read: bounded (rising then dropping back to ~0) = healthy reconcile cadence. Monotonically climbing past the controller's natural re-queue interval = the controller is failing every reconcile or spinning without reaching steady state. The companion alerts in monitoring.rulesEnabled fire on this signal.

title: Redpanda Operator — Full Observability
